response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer with strengths in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but fails the hard requirements for this role. There is no Japanese language proficiency listed (required at native level), no demonstrated embedded firmware or LAN-switch experience, no evidence of Linux kernel source modification, and only minimal/dated C exposure (National Instruments, 2000–2001). The security technology stack (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, tamper detection) and switch/networking domain (L2/L3, NETCONF, SNMP, chip-vendor SDKs) are absent from the resume.
strengths: Long software engineering tenure with broad multi-language exposure
Strong testing/TDD culture and developer-tooling background
Some early-career C/C++ experience at National Instruments on a UNIX-style IDE
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — fails a stated required qualification
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or networking-stack experience (L2/L3, NETCONF, SNMP, MACsec, 802.1X)
No evidence of Linux kernel investigation or source-code modification, and C work is dated and peripheral

key_factors.factor: No Linux kernel or modern C/UNIX systems work
